message.content:
Copy all render TypeScript files from `C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow/src/report/render/` to `D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/src/render/`.

For each file, apply these import replacements:
1. `from '../../types/transcript.js'` → `from '@openmnemo/types'`
2. `from '../../types/report.js'` → `from '../types.js'`
(No other imports change — relative imports within render/ stay the same)

The files to copy are:
- archive.ts
- breadcrumb.ts
- charts.ts
- css.ts
- dashboard.ts
- goals.ts
- graph.ts
- knowledge.ts
- layout.ts
- links.ts
- markdown.ts
- projects.ts
- rss.ts
- search.ts
- toc.ts
- todos.ts
- transcript.ts
- transcript-list.ts

Read each source file, apply the substitutions, and write to the destination.

IMPORTANT: In `layout.ts`, the `clientBadge` function has a hardcoded set `KNOWN_CLIENTS = new Set(['codex', 'claude', 'gemini'])`. Update this to also include `'doubao'`:
```
const KNOWN_CLIENTS = new Set(['codex', 'claude', 'gemini', 'doubao'])
```

Write all files to `D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/report/src/render/`. Do NOT skip any file.

content.text: I'll read all the source files in parallel, then write them to the destination with the required import substitutions.

content.content:
1→/** 2→ * Archive page: renders markdown files from Memory/05_archive/. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js' 6→import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js' 7→import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js' 8→import { hasMermaidBlocks, markdownToHtml, MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } from './markdown.js' 9→import { extractToc, renderToc, injectHeadingIds, prefixTocIds } from './toc.js' 10→ 11→export type ArchiveFile = MarkdownFile 12→ 13→export function renderArchive(files: ArchiveFile[], t?: Translations): string { 14→ const nav = renderNav('archive', 1, t) 15→ const title = t?.nav.archive ?? 'Archive' 16→ 17→ if (files.length === 0) { 18→ const content = `
19→

${escHtml(title)}

20→

No archive files found in Memory/05_archive/.

21→
` 22→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav) 23→ } 24→ 25→ const sections = files 26→ .map(f => { 27→ const sectionId = slugifyName(f.filename) 28→ const tocEntries = prefixTocIds(extractToc(f.content), sectionId) 29→ const rawHtml = markdownToHtml(f.content) 30→ const htmlContent = injectHeadingIds(rawHtml, tocEntries) 31→ const toc = renderToc(tocEntries) 32→ return `
33→

${escHtml(f.title)}

34→

${escHtml(f.filename)}

35→ ${toc} 36→
${htmlContent}
37→
` 38→ }) 39→ .join('\n') 40→ 41→ const content = `
42→

${escHtml(title)}

43→

${files.length} archive file(s)

44→
45→${sections}` 46→ 47→ const hasMermaid = files.some(f => hasMermaidBlocks(f.content)) 48→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav, hasMermaid ? { extraHead: MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } : {}) 49→} 50→

1→/** 2→ * Breadcrumb navigation helper. 3→ * Renders an accessible
from a list of items. 4→ */ 5→ 6→import { escHtml } from './layout.js' 7→ 8→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 9→// Types 10→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 11→ 12→export interface BreadcrumbItem { 13→ label: string 14→ /** URL of this crumb. Omit for the current (last) item. */ 15→ url?: string 16→} 17→ 18→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 19→// Renderer 20→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 21→ 22→/** 23→ * Render a breadcrumb trail. 24→ * - Items with a `url` are rendered as links. 25→ * - The last item (current page) has no link and gets aria-current="page". 26→ * - Returns empty string when items array is empty. 27→ */ 28→export function renderBreadcrumb(items: BreadcrumbItem[]): string { 29→ if (items.length === 0) return '' 30→ 31→ const parts = items.map((item, i) => { 32→ const isLast = i === items.length - 1 33→ if (isLast || !item.url) { 34→ const ariaCurrent = isLast ? ' aria-current="page"' : '' 35→ return `${escHtml(item.label)}` 36→ } 37→ return `${escHtml(item.label)}` 38→ }) 39→ 40→ return `
${parts.join('›')}
` 41→} 42→

1→/** 2→ * SVG chart renderers. All inline SVG, no external libraries. 3→ * Edge cases: all-zero data renders gracefully. 4→ */ 5→ 6→import { escHtml } from './layout.js' 7→import { isoWeekKey } from '../stats.js' 8→ 9→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 10→// Color constants 11→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 12→ 13→const HEATMAP_COLORS = ['#161b22', '#0e4429', '#006d32', '#26a641', '#39d353'] 14→const CLIENT_COLORS: Record = { 15→ codex: '#388bfd', 16→ claude: '#bc8cff', 17→ gemini: '#3fb950', 18→} 19→const FALLBACK_COLORS = ['#58a6ff', '#f78166', '#d29922', '#3fb950', '#bc8cff', '#ff7b72'] 20→ 21→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 22→// Heatmap (GitHub-style, last 365 days) 23→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 24→ 25→export function renderHeatmap(dayBuckets: Record): string { 26→ const today = new Date() 27→ today.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0) 28→ 29→ // Start from the Sunday before the week that was ~52 weeks ago 30→ const startDate = new Date(today) 31→ startDate.setDate(startDate.getDate() - 364) 32→ startDate.setDate(startDate.getDate() - startDate.getDay()) 33→ 34→ const cellSize = 11 35→ const cellGap = 3 36→ const cellStep = cellSize + cellGap 37→ const leftPad = 28 // space for Mon/Wed/Fri day labels 38→ const topPad = 18 // space for month labels (unused but keeps top padding) 39→ 40→ // Build exactly 53 weeks 41→ const weeks: Array> = [] 42→ const cur = new Date(startDate) 43→ 44→ while (weeks.length < 53) { 45→ const week: Array<{ date: string; count: number; future: boolean }> = [] 46→ for (let d = 0; d < 7; d++) { 47→ const dateStr = toDateStr(cur) 48→ const isFuture = cur > today 49→ week.push({ date: dateStr, count: isFuture ? 0 : (dayBuckets[dateStr] ?? 0), future: isFuture }) 50→ cur.setDate(cur.getDate() + 1) 51→ } 52→ weeks.push(week) 53→ } 54→ 55→ const width = weeks.length * cellStep + leftPad 56→ const height = 7 * cellStep + topPad + 4 57→ 58→ const dayLabels = ['', 'Mon', '', 'Wed', '', 'Fri', ''] 59→ let cells = '' 60→ 61→ for (let w = 0; w < weeks.length; w++) { 62→ const week = weeks[w] 63→ if (!week) continue 64→ for (let d = 0; d < 7; d++) { 65→ const cell = week[d] 66→ if (!cell) continue 67→ const x = leftPad + w * cellStep 68→ const y = topPad + d * cellStep 69→ const color = cell.future ? '#0d1117' : getHeatColor(cell.count) 70→ const title = cell.date ? `${escHtml(cell.date)}: ${cell.count} session(s)` : '' 71→ cells += `` 72→ if (title) cells += `${title}` 73→ cells += `` 74→ } 75→ } 76→ 77→ let labels = '' 78→ for (let d = 0; d < 7; d++) { 79→ const label = dayLabels[d] 80→ if (!label) continue 81→ const y = topPad + d * cellStep + cellSize - 1 82→ labels += `${label}` 83→ } 84→ 85→ return ` 89→
Less 90→ ${HEATMAP_COLORS.map(c => ``).join('')} 91→More
` 92→} 93→ 94→function getHeatColor(count: number): string { 95→ if (count === 0) return HEATMAP_COLORS[0] ?? '#161b22' 96→ if (count === 1) return HEATMAP_COLORS[1] ?? '#0e4429' 97→ if (count <= 3) return HEATMAP_COLORS[2] ?? '#006d32' 98→ if (count <= 6) return HEATMAP_COLORS[3] ?? '#26a641' 99→ return HEATMAP_COLORS[4] ?? '#39d353' 100→} 101→ 102→function toDateStr(d: Date): string { 103→ const y = d.getFullYear() 104→ const m = String(d.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0') 105→ const day = String(d.getDate()).padStart(2, '0') 106→ return `${y}-${m}-${day}` 107→} 108→ 109→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 110→// Client distribution doughnut 111→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 112→ 113→export function renderClientDoughnut(clientCounts: Record): string { 114→ const cx = 90 115→ const cy = 90 116→ const r = 60 117→ const innerR = 38 118→ 119→ const entries = Object.entries(clientCounts).filter(([, v]) => v > 0) 120→ const total = entries.reduce((s, [, v]) => s + v, 0) 121→ 122→ if (total === 0 || entries.length === 0) { 123→ return emptyDoughnut(cx, cy, r) 124→ } 125→ 126→ const circumference = 2 * Math.PI * r 127→ let offset = 0 128→ let segments = '' 129→ const legendItems: string[] = [] 130→ 131→ entries.forEach(([client, count], i) => { 132→ const fraction = count / total 133→ const dashLen = fraction * circumference 134→ const dashGap = circumference - dashLen 135→ const color = CLIENT_COLORS[client] ?? FALLBACK_COLORS[i % FALLBACK_COLORS.length] ?? '#58a6ff' 136→ segments += `${escHtml(client)}: ${count} (${(fraction * 100).toFixed(0)}%)` 145→ offset += fraction 146→ 147→ const pct = (fraction * 100).toFixed(0) 148→ legendItems.push( 149→ `` + 150→ `${escHtml(client)}` + 151→ `${count} (${pct}%)`, 152→ ) 153→ return undefined 154→ }) 155→ 156→ const legendX = cx * 2 + 20 157→ const width = legendX + 180 158→ const height = cy * 2 159→ 160→ return `` 172→} 173→ 174→function emptyDoughnut(cx: number, cy: number, r: number): string { 175→ const width = cx * 2 + 220 176→ const height = cy * 2 177→ return `` 182→} 183→ 184→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 185→// Weekly messages line chart (last 52 weeks) 186→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 187→ 188→export function renderWeeklyLine(weekBuckets: Record): string { 189→ const W = 580 190→ const H = 140 191→ const padL = 38 192→ const padR = 12 193→ const padT = 12 194→ const padB = 24 195→ const plotW = W - padL - padR 196→ const plotH = H - padT - padB 197→ 198→ // Build last 52 weeks array 199→ const weeks = getLast52Weeks() 200→ const data = weeks.map(w => weekBuckets[w] ?? 0) 201→ const maxVal = Math.max(...data, 1) 202→ 203→ const points = data.map((v, i) => { 204→ const x = data.length > 1 205→ ? padL + (i / (data.length - 1)) * plotW 206→ : padL + plotW / 2 207→ const y = padT + plotH - (v / maxVal) * plotH 208→ return { x, y } 209→ }) 210→ 211→ // Build filled area path 212→ const linePoints = points.map(p => `${p.x.toFixed(1)},${p.y.toFixed(1)}`).join(' ') 213→ const firstX = points[0]?.x ?? padL 214→ const lastX = points[points.length - 1]?.x ?? (padL + plotW) 215→ const baseY = padT + plotH 216→ 217→ const areaPath = 218→ `M${firstX.toFixed(1)},${baseY} ` + 219→ points.map(p => `L${p.x.toFixed(1)},${p.y.toFixed(1)}`).join(' ') + 220→ ` L${lastX.toFixed(1)},${baseY} Z` 221→ 222→ // Y axis labels 223→ const yLabels = [0, Math.round(maxVal / 2), maxVal] 224→ .map(v => { 225→ const y = padT + plotH - (v / maxVal) * plotH 226→ return `${v}` 227→ }) 228→ .join('') 229→ 230→ // X axis: show first and last week labels 231→ const firstWeek = weeks[0] ?? '' 232→ const lastWeek = weeks[weeks.length - 1] ?? '' 233→ const xLabels = 234→ `${escHtml(firstWeek)}` + 235→ `${escHtml(lastWeek)}` 236→ 237→ return `` 251→} 252→ 253→function getLast52Weeks(): string[] { 254→ const weeks: string[] = [] 255→ const today = new Date() 256→ today.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0) 257→ for (let i = 51; i >= 0; i--) { 258→ const d = new Date(today) 259→ d.setDate(d.getDate() - i * 7) 260→ weeks.push(isoWeekKey(d)) 261→ } 262→ return weeks 263→} 264→ 265→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 266→// Top tools horizontal bar chart 267→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 268→ 269→export function renderToolBar(toolCounts: Record): string { 270→ const entries = Object.entries(toolCounts) 271→ .sort(([, a], [, b]) => b - a) 272→ .slice(0, 10) 273→ 274→ if (entries.length === 0) { 275→ return `` 278→ } 279→ 280→ const W = 500 281→ const rowH = 22 282→ const rowGap = 6 283→ const labelW = 130 284→ const barArea = W - labelW - 60 285→ const H = entries.length * (rowH + rowGap) + 10 286→ const maxCount = entries[0]?.[1] ?? 1 287→ 288→ const bars = entries 289→ .map(([name, count], i) => { 290→ const y = i * (rowH + rowGap) + 5 291→ const barW = Math.max(2, (count / maxCount) * barArea) 292→ const color = FALLBACK_COLORS[i % FALLBACK_COLORS.length] ?? '#58a6ff' 293→ return ( 294→ `${escHtml(name)}` + 295→ `` + 296→ `${escHtml(name)}: ${count}` + 297→ `${count}` 298→ ) 299→ }) 300→ .join('') 301→ 302→ return `` 305→} 306→

1→/** 2→ * Dashboard page renderer: stat cards + SVG charts + recent sessions. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js' 6→import type { ReportStats } from '../../types/report.js' 7→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js' 8→import { clientBadge, escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, transcriptUrlFromRoot } from './layout.js' 9→import { renderHeatmap, renderClientDoughnut, renderWeeklyLine, renderToolBar } from './charts.js' 10→ 11→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 12→// Dashboard 13→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 14→ 15→export function renderDashboard(stats: ReportStats, manifests: ManifestEntry[], t?: Translations): string { 16→ const nav = renderNav('dashboard', 0, t) 17→ const content = [ 18→ renderPageHeader(stats, t), 19→ renderStatsCards(stats, t), 20→ renderCharts(stats), 21→ renderRecentSessions(manifests, t), 22→ ].join('\n') 23→ 24→ const lang = t ? detectLang(t) : 'en' 25→ return htmlShell(t?.dashboard.title ?? 'Dashboard', content, nav, '', lang) 26→} 27→ 28→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 29→// Internal renderers 30→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 31→ 32→function renderPageHeader(stats: ReportStats, t?: Translations): string { 33→ const from = stats.dateRange.from.slice(0, 10) || '—' 34→ const to = stats.dateRange.to.slice(0, 10) || '—' 35→ const title = t?.dashboard.title ?? 'Memory Dashboard' 36→ const subtitle = (t?.dashboard.subtitle ?? 'Activity from {from} to {to}') 37→ .replace('{from}', from) 38→ .replace('{to}', to) 39→ return `
40→

${escHtml(title)}

41→

${escHtml(subtitle)}

42→
` 43→} 44→ 45→function renderStatsCards(stats: ReportStats, t?: Translations): string { 46→ const cards = [ 47→ { value: fmtNum(stats.totalSessions), label: t?.dashboard.sessions ?? 'Sessions' }, 48→ { value: fmtNum(stats.totalMessages), label: t?.dashboard.messages ?? 'Messages' }, 49→ { value: fmtNum(stats.totalToolEvents), label: t?.dashboard.toolEvents ?? 'Tool Events' }, 50→ { value: fmtNum(stats.activeDays), label: t?.dashboard.activeDays ?? 'Active Days' }, 51→ ] 52→ 53→ const html = cards 54→ .map( 55→ c => `
56→
${escHtml(c.label)}
57→
${c.value}
58→
`, 59→ ) 60→ .join('') 61→ 62→ return `
${html}
` 63→} 64→ 65→function renderCharts(stats: ReportStats): string { 66→ const heatmap = renderHeatmap(stats.dayBuckets) 67→ const doughnut = renderClientDoughnut(stats.clientCounts) 68→ const line = renderWeeklyLine(stats.weekBuckets) 69→ const bar = renderToolBar(stats.toolCounts) 70→ 71→ return `
72→
73→
Activity (last 365 days)
74→ ${heatmap} 75→
76→
77→
Client Distribution
78→ ${doughnut} 79→
80→
81→
Messages / Week (last 52 weeks)
82→ ${line} 83→
84→
85→
Top 10 Tools
86→ ${bar} 87→
88→
` 89→} 90→ 91→function renderRecentSessions(manifests: ManifestEntry[], t?: Translations): string { 92→ const recent = [...manifests] 93→ .sort((a, b) => b.started_at.localeCompare(a.started_at)) 94→ .slice(0, 10) 95→ 96→ const heading = t?.dashboard.recentSessions ?? 'Recent Sessions' 97→ const clientLabel = t?.sessions.client ?? 'Client' 98→ const dateLabel = t?.sessions.date ?? 'Date' 99→ const msgsLabel = t?.sessions.msgs ?? 'Msgs' 100→ const toolsLabel = t?.sessions.tools ?? 'Tools' 101→ 102→ if (recent.length === 0) { 103→ return `

${escHtml(t?.sessions.noSessions ?? 'No sessions imported yet.')}

` 104→ } 105→ 106→ const rows = recent 107→ .map(m => { 108→ const url = transcriptUrlFromRoot(m) 109→ const date = m.started_at.slice(0, 10) 110→ const badge = clientBadge(m.client) 111→ const msgs = m.message_count 112→ const tools = m.tool_event_count 113→ const summary = '' 114→ const meta = `${escHtml(m.client)} · ${escHtml(date)} · ${msgs} msgs` 115→ return ` 116→ ${badge} 117→ ${escHtml(m.title || m.session_id)} 118→ ${escHtml(date)} 119→ ${msgs} 120→ ${tools} 121→` 122→ }) 123→ .join('') 124→ 125→ return `

${escHtml(heading)}

126→
127→ 128→ 129→ 130→ 131→ 132→ 133→${rows} 134→
${escHtml(clientLabel)}Title${escHtml(dateLabel)}${escHtml(msgsLabel)}${escHtml(toolsLabel)}
135→
` 136→} 137→ 138→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 139→// Helpers 140→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 141→ 142→function fmtNum(n: number): string { 143→ return n.toLocaleString('en-US') 144→} 145→ 146→function detectLang(t: Translations): string { 147→ // Heuristic: check if nav.dashboard is Chinese 148→ return /[\u4e00-\u9fff]/.test(t.nav.dashboard) ? 'zh-CN' : 'en' 149→} 150→

1→/** 2→ * Goals page: renders markdown files from Memory/01_goals/. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js' 6→import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js' 7→import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js' 8→import { hasMermaidBlocks, markdownToHtml, MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } from './markdown.js' 9→import { extractToc, renderToc, injectHeadingIds, prefixTocIds } from './toc.js' 10→ 11→export type GoalFile = MarkdownFile 12→ 13→export function renderGoals(files: GoalFile[], t?: Translations): string { 14→ const nav = renderNav('goals', 1, t) 15→ const title = t?.nav.goals ?? 'Goals' 16→ 17→ if (files.length === 0) { 18→ const content = `
19→

${escHtml(title)}

20→

No goal files found in Memory/01_goals/.

21→
` 22→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav) 23→ } 24→ 25→ const sections = files 26→ .map(f => { 27→ const sectionId = slugifyName(f.filename) 28→ const tocEntries = prefixTocIds(extractToc(f.content), sectionId) 29→ const rawHtml = markdownToHtml(f.content) 30→ const htmlContent = injectHeadingIds(rawHtml, tocEntries) 31→ const toc = renderToc(tocEntries) 32→ return `
33→

${escHtml(f.title)}

34→

${escHtml(f.filename)}

35→ ${toc} 36→
${htmlContent}
37→
` 38→ }) 39→ .join('\n') 40→ 41→ const content = `
42→

${escHtml(title)}

43→

${files.length} goal file(s)

44→
45→${sections}` 46→ 47→ const hasMermaid = files.some(f => hasMermaidBlocks(f.content)) 48→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav, hasMermaid ? { extraHead: MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } : {}) 49→} 50→

1→/** 2→ * Knowledge page: renders markdown files from Memory/04_knowledge/. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js' 6→import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js' 7→import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js' 8→import { hasMermaidBlocks, markdownToHtml, MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } from './markdown.js' 9→import { extractToc, renderToc, injectHeadingIds, prefixTocIds } from './toc.js' 10→ 11→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 12→// Knowledge page 13→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 14→ 15→export type KnowledgeFile = MarkdownFile 16→ 17→export function renderKnowledge(files: KnowledgeFile[], t?: Translations): string { 18→ const nav = renderNav('knowledge', 1, t) 19→ const title = t?.nav.knowledge ?? 'Knowledge' 20→ 21→ if (files.length === 0) { 22→ const content = `
23→

${escHtml(title)}

24→

No knowledge files found in Memory/04_knowledge/.

25→
` 26→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav) 27→ } 28→ 29→ const hasMermaid = files.some(f => hasMermaidBlocks(f.content)) 30→ 31→ const sections = files 32→ .map(f => { 33→ const sectionId = slugifyName(f.filename) 34→ const tocEntries = prefixTocIds(extractToc(f.content), sectionId) 35→ const rawHtml = markdownToHtml(f.content) 36→ const htmlContent = injectHeadingIds(rawHtml, tocEntries) 37→ const toc = renderToc(tocEntries) 38→ return `
39→

${escHtml(f.title)}

40→

${escHtml(f.filename)}

41→ ${toc} 42→
${htmlContent}
43→
` 44→ }) 45→ .join('\n') 46→ 47→ const content = `
48→

${escHtml(title)}

49→

${files.length} knowledge file(s)

50→
51→${sections}` 52→ 53→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav, hasMermaid ? { extraHead: MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } : {}) 54→} 55→

1→/** 2→ * Projects page: groups sessions by their working directory (last segment). 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js' 6→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js' 7→import { clientBadge, escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav } from './layout.js' 8→ 9→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 10→// Project extraction 11→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 12→ 13→export function extractProject(cwd: string): string { 14→ if (!cwd) return 'unknown' 15→ return cwd.split(/[/\\]/).filter(Boolean).at(-1) ?? 'unknown' 16→} 17→ 18→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 19→// Projects page 20→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 21→ 22→export function renderProjects(manifests: ManifestEntry[], t?: Translations): string { 23→ const nav = renderNav('projects', 1, t) 24→ const title = t?.projects.title ?? 'Projects' 25→ 26→ if (manifests.length === 0) { 27→ const content = `
28→

${escHtml(title)}

29→

${escHtml(t?.projects.noProjects ?? 'No projects found.')}

30→
` 31→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav) 32→ } 33→ 34→ // Group by project name 35→ const projectMap = new Map() 36→ for (const m of manifests) { 37→ const proj = extractProject(m.cwd) 38→ const group = projectMap.get(proj) 39→ if (group) { 40→ group.push(m) 41→ } else { 42→ projectMap.set(proj, [m]) 43→ } 44→ } 45→ 46→ // Sort by session count desc 47→ const sorted = [...projectMap.entries()].sort((a, b) => b[1].length - a[1].length) 48→ 49→ const sessionLabel = t?.projects.sessions ?? 'sessions' 50→ 51→ const cards = sorted.map(([name, sessions]) => { 52→ const count = sessions.length 53→ const lastActive = sessions 54→ .map(s => s.started_at) 55→ .sort() 56→ .at(-1) 57→ ?.slice(0, 10) ?? '—' 58→ 59→ const clients = [...new Set(sessions.map(s => s.client))] 60→ const badges = clients.map(c => clientBadge(c)).join(' ') 61→ 62→ return `
63→
${escHtml(name)}
64→
65→ ${count} ${escHtml(sessionLabel)} · last active ${escHtml(lastActive)} 66→
67→
${badges}
68→
` 69→ }).join('\n') 70→ 71→ const content = `
72→

${escHtml(title)}

73→

${sorted.length} project(s)

74→
75→
76→${cards} 77→
` 78→ 79→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav) 80→} 81→

1→/** 2→ * Minimal Markdown → HTML converter. 3→ * Handles: headings, bold, italic, inline code, fenced code blocks, 4→ * lists, blockquotes, callouts (> [!type]), mermaid diagrams. 5→ * All content is HTML-escaped before applying structural tags. 6→ */ 7→ 8→import { escHtml } from './layout.js' 9→ 10→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 11→// Callout configuration 12→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 13→ 14→const CALLOUT_ICONS: Record = { 15→ note: '📝', 16→ tip: '💡', 17→ important: '❗', 18→ warning: '⚠️', 19→ caution: '🔥', 20→ info: 'ℹ️', 21→ success: '✅', 22→ error: '❌', 23→} 24→ 25→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 26→// Mermaid detection (separate from conversion) 27→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 28→ 29→/** Returns true if the markdown source contains at least one ```mermaid block. */ 30→export function hasMermaidBlocks(md: string): boolean { 31→ return /^```mermaid\s*$/m.test(md) 32→} 33→ 34→/** 35→ * CDN script tag to inject on pages containing mermaid diagrams. 36→ * Pinned to mermaid@11 via jsDelivr with SRI integrity hash. 37→ */ 38→export const MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT = `` 42→ 43→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 44→// Main converter 45→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 46→ 47→export function markdownToHtml(md: string): string { 48→ const lines = md.split('\n') 49→ const output: string[] = [] 50→ let i = 0 51→ let inList = false 52→ let listType: 'ul' | 'ol' | null = null 53→ 54→ function closeList() { 55→ if (inList) { 56→ output.push(listType === 'ol' ? '' : '') 57→ inList = false 58→ listType = null 59→ } 60→ } 61→ 62→ while (i < lines.length) { 63→ const line = lines[i] ?? '' 64→ 65→ // Fenced code block (including mermaid) 66→ if (line.startsWith('```')) { 67→ closeList() 68→ const lang = line.slice(3).trim() 69→ const codeLines: string[] = [] 70→ i++ 71→ while (i < lines.length && !(lines[i] ?? '').startsWith('```')) { 72→ codeLines.push(lines[i] ?? '') 73→ i++ 74→ } 75→ const codeContent = codeLines.join('\n') 76→ if (lang === 'mermaid') { 77→ // Mermaid: render as 
 — processed client-side by mermaid.js
    78→        output.push(`
${escHtml(codeContent)}
`)
    79→      } else {
    80→        output.push(`
${escHtml(codeContent)}
`)
    81→      }
    82→      i++
    83→      continue
    84→    }
    85→
    86→    // YAML front matter (skip)
    87→    if (i === 0 && line === '---') {
    88→      i++
    89→      while (i < lines.length && (lines[i] ?? '') !== '---') i++
    90→      i++ // skip closing ---
    91→      continue
    92→    }
    93→
    94→    // Headings
    95→    const headingMatch = line.match(/^(#{1,6})\s+(.*)$/)
    96→    if (headingMatch) {
    97→      closeList()
    98→      const level = headingMatch[1]?.length ?? 1
    99→      const text = headingMatch[2] ?? ''
   100→      output.push(`${applyInline(text)}`)
   101→      i++
   102→      continue
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    // Horizontal rule
   106→    if (/^---+$/.test(line.trim())) {
   107→      closeList()
   108→      output.push('
')
   109→      i++
   110→      continue
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    // Ordered list
   114→    const olMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)\d+\.\s+(.+)$/)
   115→    if (olMatch) {
   116→      if (!inList || listType !== 'ol') {
   117→        closeList()
   118→        output.push('
    ')
   119→        inList = true
   120→        listType = 'ol'
   121→      }
   122→      output.push(`
  1. ${applyInline(olMatch[2] ?? '')}
    2. `)
   123→      i++
   124→      continue
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    // Unordered list
   128→    const ulMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)[-*+]\s+(.+)$/)
   129→    if (ulMatch) {
   130→      if (!inList || listType !== 'ul') {
   131→        closeList()
   132→        output.push('
      ')
   133→        inList = true
   134→        listType = 'ul'
   135→      }
   136→      output.push(`
    • ${applyInline(ulMatch[2] ?? '')}
      • `)
   137→      i++
   138→      continue
   139→    }
   140→
   141→    // Blockquote / Callout
   142→    if (line.startsWith('> ') || line === '>') {
   143→      closeList()
   144→      const bqLines: string[] = []
   145→      while (i < lines.length && ((lines[i] ?? '').startsWith('> ') || (lines[i] ?? '') === '>')) {
   146→        bqLines.push((lines[i] ?? '').replace(/^> ?/, ''))
   147→        i++
   148→      }
   149→      output.push(renderBlockquote(bqLines))
   150→      continue
   151→    }
   152→
   153→    // Blank line
   154→    if (!line.trim()) {
   155→      closeList()
   156→      i++
   157→      continue
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    // Paragraph
   161→    closeList()
   162→    output.push(`
      ${applyInline(line)}
      `)
   163→    i++
   164→  }
   165→
   166→  closeList()
   167→  return output.join('\n')
   168→}
   169→
   170→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   171→// Blockquote / Callout renderer
   172→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   173→
   174→function renderBlockquote(innerLines: string[]): string {
   175→  const firstLine = innerLines[0] ?? ''
   176→  const calloutMatch = firstLine.match(/^\[!(note|tip|important|warning|caution|info|success|error)\]$/i)
   177→
   178→  if (calloutMatch) {
   179→    const type = calloutMatch[1]!.toLowerCase()
   180→    const icon = CALLOUT_ICONS[type] ?? '💬'
   181→    const title = type.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + type.slice(1)
   182→    const bodyLines = innerLines.slice(1).filter(l => l !== '')
   183→    const body = bodyLines.map(l => `
      ${applyInline(l)}
      `).join('\n')
   184→    return `
      
   185→  
      ${icon} ${escHtml(title)}
      
   186→  
      ${body}
      
   187→
      `
   188→  }
   189→
   190→  // Regular blockquote
   191→  const inner = innerLines.map(l => `
      ${applyInline(l)}
      `).join('\n')
   192→  return `
      ${inner}
      `
   193→}
   194→
   195→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   196→// Inline formatting
   197→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   198→
   199→function applyInline(text: string): string {
   200→  // Process links before HTML-escaping to avoid double-escaping href/label
   201→  const withLinks = text.replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\(([^)]+)\)/g, (_, label: string, href: string) => {
   202→    return `${escHtml(label)}`
   203→  })
   204→  // Escape remaining text, then apply inline patterns on already-safe content
   205→  // Split on the  tags we just inserted so they are not re-escaped
   206→  const parts = withLinks.split(/(]+>.*?<\/a>)/g)
   207→  const processed = parts
   208→    .map((part, i) => {
   209→      if (i % 2 === 1) return part // already-generated  tag, pass through
   210→      const escaped = escHtml(part)
   211→      return escaped
   212→        .replace(/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g, '$1')
   213→        .replace(/\*(.+?)\*/g, '$1')
   214→        .replace(/__(.+?)__/g, '$1')
   215→        .replace(/_(.+?)_/g, '$1')
   216→        .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '$1')
   217→    })
   218→    .join('')
   219→  return processed
   220→}
   221→

1→/**
     2→ * RSS 2.0 feed generator for MemoryTree sessions.
     3→ * Generates a valid feed.xml file.
     4→ * When baseUrl is empty, item links are omitted gracefully.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js'
     8→import { transcriptUrlFromRoot } from './layout.js'
     9→
    10→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    11→// XML escaping
    12→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    13→
    14→function xmlEsc(text: string): string {
    15→  return text
    16→    .replace(/&/g, '&')
    17→    .replace(/</g, '<')
    18→    .replace(/>/g, '>')
    19→    .replace(/"/g, '"')
    20→    .replace(/'/g, ''')
    21→}
    22→
    23→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24→// RSS renderer
    25→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→
    27→/**
    28→ * Generate an RSS 2.0 feed from session manifests.
    29→ *
    30→ * @param manifests  All session manifests (sorted by started_at descending by caller)
    31→ * @param summaries  Map of session_id → AI summary text
    32→ * @param baseUrl    Absolute base URL (e.g. 'https://memory.example.com').
    33→ *                   When empty, item <link> and channel <link> are omitted.
    34→ */
    35→export function renderRssFeed(
    36→  manifests: ManifestEntry[],
    37→  summaries: Record<string, string>,
    38→  baseUrl: string,
    39→): string {
    40→  const MAX_ITEMS = 100
    41→  const sorted = [...manifests]
    42→    .sort((a, b) => b.started_at.localeCompare(a.started_at))
    43→    .slice(0, MAX_ITEMS)
    44→
    45→  const channelLink = baseUrl ? `\n  <link>${xmlEsc(baseUrl)}</link>` : ''
    46→
    47→  const items = sorted.map(m => {
    48→    const title = m.title || m.session_id
    49→    const summary = summaries[m.session_id] ?? ''
    50→    const pubDate = toRfc2822(m.started_at)
    51→    const relUrl = transcriptUrlFromRoot(m)
    52→    const absUrl = baseUrl ? `${baseUrl.replace(/\/$/, '')}/${relUrl}` : ''
    53→    const linkTag = absUrl ? `\n    <link>${xmlEsc(absUrl)}</link>` : ''
    54→    const guidTag = absUrl
    55→      ? `\n    <guid isPermaLink="true">${xmlEsc(absUrl)}</guid>`
    56→      : `\n    <guid isPermaLink="false">${xmlEsc(m.session_id)}</guid>`
    57→    const descTag = summary
    58→      ? `\n    <description>${xmlEsc(summary.slice(0, 500))}</description>`
    59→      : ''
    60→    return `  <item>
    61→    <title>${xmlEsc(title)}</title>${linkTag}${guidTag}
    62→    <pubDate>${xmlEsc(pubDate)}</pubDate>${descTag}
    63→  </item>`
    64→  }).join('\n')
    65→
    66→  return `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    67→<rss version="2.0" xmlns:atom="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom">
    68→<channel>
    69→  <title>MemoryTree Sessions</title>${channelLink}
    70→  <description>AI session transcripts exported by MemoryTree</description>
    71→  <generator>MemoryTree</generator>
    72→${items}
    73→</channel>
    74→</rss>`
    75→}
    76→
    77→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    78→// Helpers
    79→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    80→
    81→/** Convert ISO 8601 to RFC 2822 format (required by RSS spec). */
    82→function toRfc2822(iso: string): string {
    83→  const d = new Date(iso)
    84→  return isNaN(d.getTime()) ? new Date().toUTCString() : d.toUTCString()
    85→}
    86→

1→/** 2→ * Table of contents extraction and rendering. 3→ * Extracts h2/h3 headings from raw markdown and generates a sticky TOC nav. 4→ */ 5→ 6→import { escHtml } from './layout.js' 7→ 8→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 9→// Types 10→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 11→ 12→export interface TocEntry { 13→ level: 2 | 3 14→ text: string 15→ /** URL-safe anchor id, duplicate-free (suffixed -1, -2, …) */ 16→ id: string 17→} 18→ 19→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 20→// Extract TOC from raw markdown 21→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 22→ 23→/** 24→ * Parse h2 and h3 headings from markdown source. 25→ * Duplicate heading text gets a numeric suffix: `-1`, `-2`, … 26→ */ 27→export function extractToc(md: string): TocEntry[] { 28→ const entries: TocEntry[] = [] 29→ const seen = new Map() 30→ 31→ for (const line of md.split('\n')) { 32→ const h2 = line.match(/^## (.+)$/) 33→ const h3 = line.match(/^### (.+)$/) 34→ const match = h2 ?? h3 35→ if (!match) continue 36→ 37→ const level: 2 | 3 = h2 ? 2 : 3 38→ const text = (match[1] ?? '').trim() 39→ const base = slugifyHeading(text) 40→ 41→ const count = seen.get(base) ?? 0 42→ const id = count === 0 ? base : `${base}-${count}` 43→ seen.set(base, count + 1) 44→ 45→ entries.push({ level, text, id }) 46→ } 47→ 48→ return entries 49→} 50→ 51→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 52→// Render TOC HTML 53→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 54→ 55→/** 56→ * Render a collapsible TOC nav from extracted entries. 57→ * Returns empty string when entries is empty. 58→ */ 59→export function renderToc(entries: TocEntry[]): string { 60→ if (entries.length === 0) return '' 61→ 62→ const items = entries 63→ .map(e => { 64→ const indent = e.level === 3 ? ' style="padding-left:1.25rem"' : '' 65→ return `${escHtml(e.text)}` 66→ }) 67→ .join('\n ') 68→ 69→ return `
70→ 73→
    74→ ${items} 75→
76→
` 77→} 78→ 79→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 80→// TOC ID scoping 81→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 82→ 83→export function prefixTocIds(entries: TocEntry[], prefix: string): TocEntry[] { 84→ if (!prefix) return entries 85→ return entries.map(entry => ({ 86→ ...entry, 87→ id: `${prefix}-${entry.id}`, 88→ })) 89→} 90→ 91→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 92→// Heading anchor injection 93→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 94→ 95→/** 96→ * Post-process rendered HTML to add id attributes to heading tags so 97→ * TOC anchor links resolve correctly. 98→ * Only modifies

and

tags without existing id attributes. 99→ */ 100→export function injectHeadingIds(html: string, entries: TocEntry[]): string { 101→ if (entries.length === 0) return html 102→ 103→ // Process entries in order so duplicates match correctly 104→ const remaining = [...entries] 105→ // [\s\S]*? allows inline HTML inside headings (e.g. , ) 106→ return html.replace(/([\s\S]*?)<\/h[23]>/g, (full, levelStr, innerHtml) => { 107→ const level = parseInt(levelStr, 10) as 2 | 3 108→ // Strip HTML tags from rendered inner content to get plain text for matching 109→ const innerText = (innerHtml as string).replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim() 110→ const idx = remaining.findIndex(e => { 111→ if (e.level !== level) return false 112→ // Compare plain text: strip markdown markers from entry text, strip HTML from inner 113→ return stripInlineMarkdown(e.text) === innerText || escHtml(e.text) === (innerHtml as string).trim() 114→ }) 115→ if (idx === -1) return full 116→ const entry = remaining.splice(idx, 1)[0]! 117→ return `${innerHtml}` 118→ }) 119→} 120→ 121→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 122→// Internal helpers 123→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 124→ 125→function slugifyHeading(text: string): string { 126→ return text 127→ .toLowerCase() 128→ .replace(/[^\w\s-]/g, '') // remove non-word chars except hyphen 129→ .trim() 130→ .replace(/[\s_]+/g, '-') // spaces/underscores → hyphen 131→ .replace(/-+/g, '-') // collapse multiple hyphens 132→} 133→ 134→/** 135→ * Strip common markdown inline markers to get plain text. 136→ * Used to match TOC entries against rendered HTML heading content. 137→ */ 138→function stripInlineMarkdown(text: string): string { 139→ return text 140→ .replace(/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g, '$1') // **bold** 141→ .replace(/\*([^*]+)\*/g, '$1') // *italic* 142→ .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '$1') // `code` 143→ .replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\([^)]+\)/g, '$1') // [link](url) → text 144→ .trim() 145→} 146→

1→/** 2→ * Todos page: renders markdown files from Memory/02_todos/. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js' 6→import { escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, slugifyName } from './layout.js' 7→import type { MarkdownFile } from './layout.js' 8→import { hasMermaidBlocks, markdownToHtml, MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } from './markdown.js' 9→import { extractToc, renderToc, injectHeadingIds, prefixTocIds } from './toc.js' 10→ 11→export type TodoFile = MarkdownFile 12→ 13→export function renderTodos(files: TodoFile[], t?: Translations): string { 14→ const nav = renderNav('todos', 1, t) 15→ const title = t?.nav.todos ?? 'Todos' 16→ 17→ if (files.length === 0) { 18→ const content = `
19→

${escHtml(title)}

20→

No todo files found in Memory/02_todos/.

21→
` 22→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav) 23→ } 24→ 25→ const sections = files 26→ .map(f => { 27→ const sectionId = slugifyName(f.filename) 28→ const tocEntries = prefixTocIds(extractToc(f.content), sectionId) 29→ const rawHtml = markdownToHtml(f.content) 30→ const htmlContent = injectHeadingIds(rawHtml, tocEntries) 31→ const toc = renderToc(tocEntries) 32→ return `
33→

${escHtml(f.title)}

34→

${escHtml(f.filename)}

35→ ${toc} 36→
${htmlContent}
37→
` 38→ }) 39→ .join('\n') 40→ 41→ const content = `
42→

${escHtml(title)}

43→

${files.length} todo file(s)

44→
45→${sections}` 46→ 47→ const hasMermaid = files.some(f => hasMermaidBlocks(f.content)) 48→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav, hasMermaid ? { extraHead: MERMAID_CDN_SCRIPT } : {}) 49→} 50→

1→/** 2→ * Individual transcript page: metadata, AI summary, backlinks, messages. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js' 6→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js' 7→import { clientBadge, escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, transcriptUrlFromRoot, transcriptUrlFromTranscript } from './layout.js' 8→ 9→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 10→// Parsed message for rendering 11→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 12→ 13→export interface RenderedMessage { 14→ role: string 15→ timestamp: string 16→ text: string 17→} 18→ 19→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 20→// Transcript page 21→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 22→ 23→export function renderTranscript( 24→ messages: RenderedMessage[], 25→ manifest: ManifestEntry, 26→ summary: string, 27→ backlinks: ManifestEntry[], 28→ t?: Translations, 29→ reportBaseUrl = '', 30→): string { 31→ const nav = renderNav('transcripts', 2, t) 32→ 33→ const content = [ 34→ renderHeader(manifest, t), 35→ summary ? renderSummaryCard(summary, t) : '', 36→ backlinks.length > 0 ? renderBacklinks(backlinks, t) : '', 37→ renderMessages(messages, t), 38→ ] 39→ .filter(Boolean) 40→ .join('\n') 41→ 42→ const title = manifest.title || manifest.session_id 43→ const ogUrl = reportBaseUrl 44→ ? `${reportBaseUrl.replace(/\/$/, '')}/${transcriptUrlFromRoot(manifest)}` 45→ : '' 46→ const shellOptions = { 47→ ...(summary ? { ogDescription: summary } : {}), 48→ ...(ogUrl ? { ogUrl } : {}), 49→ } 50→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav, shellOptions) 51→} 52→ 53→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 54→// Sub-renderers 55→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 56→ 57→function renderHeader(m: ManifestEntry, t?: Translations): string { 58→ const badge = clientBadge(m.client) 59→ const date = m.started_at.slice(0, 10) 60→ const time = m.started_at.slice(11, 19) 61→ const clientLabel = t?.transcript.client ?? 'Client' 62→ const sessionIdLabel = t?.transcript.sessionId ?? 'Session ID' 63→ const msgsLabel = t?.transcript.messages ?? 'Messages' 64→ const toolEventsLabel = t?.transcript.toolEvents ?? 'Tool Events' 65→ const branchLabel = t?.transcript.branch ?? 'Branch' 66→ const cwdLabel = t?.transcript.workingDir ?? 'Working Dir' 67→ const shaLabel = t?.transcript.sha256 ?? 'SHA-256' 68→ 69→ return `
70→

${escHtml(m.title || m.session_id)}

71→
72→ 73→ 74→ 75→ 76→ 77→ 78→ 79→ 80→ 81→ 82→ 83→
${escHtml(clientLabel)}${badge}
Date${escHtml(date)} ${escHtml(time)}
${escHtml(sessionIdLabel)}${escHtml(m.session_id)}
${escHtml(msgsLabel)}${m.message_count}
${escHtml(toolEventsLabel)}${m.tool_event_count}
${escHtml(branchLabel)}${escHtml(m.branch || '—')}
${escHtml(cwdLabel)}${escHtml(m.cwd || '—')}
${escHtml(shaLabel)}${escHtml(m.raw_sha256.slice(0, 16))}…
` 84→} 85→ 86→function renderSummaryCard(summary: string, t?: Translations): string { 87→ const label = t?.transcript.aiSummary ?? 'AI Summary' 88→ return `
89→
${escHtml(label)}
90→
${escHtml(summary)}
91→
` 92→} 93→ 94→function renderBacklinks(backlinks: ManifestEntry[], t?: Translations): string { 95→ const heading = t?.transcript.referencedBy ?? 'Referenced By' 96→ const items = backlinks 97→ .map(m => { 98→ const url = transcriptHref(m) 99→ return `
  • ${escHtml(m.title || m.session_id)}
    • ` 100→ }) 101→ .join('') 102→ return `
    103→
    ${escHtml(heading)} ${backlinks.length} session(s)
    104→
      ${items}
    105→
    ` 106→} 107→ 108→function renderMessages(messages: RenderedMessage[], t?: Translations): string { 109→ const heading = t?.transcript.messages ?? 'Messages' 110→ const noMessages = t?.transcript.noMessages ?? 'No messages available for this session.' 111→ if (messages.length === 0) { 112→ return `
    ${escHtml(noMessages)}
    ` 113→ } 114→ 115→ const rendered = messages 116→ .map(msg => { 117→ const roleClass = msg.role === 'user' ? 'message-user' : 'message-assistant' 118→ const ts = msg.timestamp ? `${escHtml(msg.timestamp.slice(11, 19))}` : '' 119→ const body = renderMessageBody(msg.text) 120→ return `
    121→
    122→ ${escHtml(msg.role)}${ts} 123→
    124→
    ${body}
    125→
    ` 126→ }) 127→ .join('\n') 128→ 129→ return `

    ${escHtml(heading)}

    130→
    ${rendered}
    ` 131→} 132→ 133→/** 134→ * Render message text to HTML with code block support. 135→ * Input text is plain text from clean markdown. 136→ */ 137→function renderMessageBody(text: string): string { 138→ // Process code blocks first (they contain preformatted content) 139→ const parts: string[] = [] 140→ const codeBlockRe = /```[\w]*\n?([\s\S]*?)```/g 141→ let lastIndex = 0 142→ let match: RegExpExecArray | null 143→ 144→ while ((match = codeBlockRe.exec(text)) !== null) { 145→ if (match.index > lastIndex) { 146→ parts.push(renderInlineText(text.slice(lastIndex, match.index))) 147→ } 148→ const code = match[1] ?? '' 149→ parts.push(`
    ${escHtml(code)}
    `) 150→ lastIndex = match.index + match[0].length 151→ } 152→ 153→ if (lastIndex < text.length) { 154→ parts.push(renderInlineText(text.slice(lastIndex))) 155→ } 156→ 157→ return parts.join('') 158→} 159→ 160→/** Render inline text (paragraphs + inline formatting). */ 161→function renderInlineText(text: string): string { 162→ const paragraphs = text.split(/\n{2,}/).filter(p => p.trim()) 163→ return paragraphs 164→ .map(p => `

    ${applyInlineMarkdown(escHtml(p.trim()))}

    `) 165→ .join('') 166→} 167→ 168→/** Apply inline markdown to already-HTML-escaped text. */ 169→function applyInlineMarkdown(html: string): string { 170→ return html 171→ .replace(/\*\*(.+?)\*\*/g, '$1') 172→ .replace(/\*(.+?)\*/g, '$1') 173→ .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '$1') 174→ .replace(/\n/g, '
    ') 175→} 176→ 177→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 178→// Helper 179→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 180→ 181→function transcriptHref(m: ManifestEntry): string { 182→ return transcriptUrlFromTranscript(m) 183→} 184→

1→/** 2→ * Session list page: tabular index of all imported transcripts, with client tab filtering. 3→ */ 4→ 5→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../../types/transcript.js' 6→import type { Translations } from '../i18n/types.js' 7→import { clientBadge, escHtml, htmlShell, renderNav, transcriptUrlFromRoot } from './layout.js' 8→import { renderTagBadges } from '../tags.js' 9→ 10→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 11→// Transcript list 12→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 13→ 14→export function renderTranscriptList(manifests: ManifestEntry[], t?: Translations, summaries?: Record, tags?: Record): string { 15→ const nav = renderNav('transcripts', 1, t) 16→ 17→ const sorted = [...manifests].sort((a, b) => b.started_at.localeCompare(a.started_at)) 18→ 19→ const title = t?.sessions.title ?? 'Sessions' 20→ const clientLabel = t?.sessions.client ?? 'Client' 21→ const dateLabel = t?.sessions.date ?? 'Date' 22→ const idLabel = t?.sessions.id ?? 'ID' 23→ const msgsLabel = t?.sessions.msgs ?? 'Msgs' 24→ const toolsLabel = t?.sessions.tools ?? 'Tools' 25→ const allLabel = t?.sessions.all ?? 'All' 26→ 27→ if (sorted.length === 0) { 28→ const content = `
29→

${escHtml(title)}

30→

${escHtml(t?.sessions.noSessions ?? 'No sessions imported yet.')}

31→
` 32→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav) 33→ } 34→ 35→ // Collect unique clients 36→ const clientSet = new Set() 37→ for (const m of sorted) clientSet.add(m.client) 38→ const clients = [...clientSet].sort() 39→ 40→ // Tab bar 41→ const tabBar = renderTabBar(allLabel, clients, sorted) 42→ 43→ const rows = sorted 44→ .map(m => { 45→ const url = transcriptHref(m) 46→ const date = m.started_at.slice(0, 10) 47→ const time = m.started_at.slice(11, 16) 48→ const badge = clientBadge(m.client) 49→ const summary = summaries?.[m.session_id] ?? '' 50→ const sessionTags = tags?.[m.session_id] ?? [] 51→ const tagBadges = renderTagBadges(sessionTags) 52→ const meta = `${escHtml(m.client)} · ${escHtml(date)} · ${m.message_count} msgs` 53→ return ` 54→ ${badge} 55→ ${escHtml(m.title || m.session_id)}${tagBadges} 56→ ${escHtml(date)} ${escHtml(time)} 57→ ${escHtml(m.session_id.slice(0, 8))} 58→ ${m.message_count} 59→ ${m.tool_event_count} 60→` 61→ }) 62→ .join('') 63→ 64→ const filterJs = `` 86→ 87→ const content = `
88→

${escHtml(title)}

89→

${sorted.length} session(s) imported

90→
91→${tabBar} 92→
93→ 94→ 95→ 96→ 97→ 98→ 99→ 100→ 101→ 102→${rows} 103→
${escHtml(clientLabel)}Title${escHtml(dateLabel)}${escHtml(idLabel)}${escHtml(msgsLabel)}${escHtml(toolsLabel)}
104→
` 105→ 106→ return htmlShell(title, content, nav, filterJs) 107→} 108→ 109→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 110→// Tab bar 111→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 112→ 113→function renderTabBar(allLabel: string, clients: string[], manifests: ManifestEntry[]): string { 114→ const total = manifests.length 115→ const tabs = [ 116→ ``, 117→ ...clients.map(client => { 118→ const count = manifests.filter(m => m.client === client).length 119→ return `` 120→ }), 121→ ] 122→ return `
${tabs.join('')}
` 123→} 124→ 125→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 126→// Helper 127→// --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 128→ 129→function transcriptHref(m: ManifestEntry): string { 130→ // From transcripts/index.html, the URL is relative to the transcripts/ dir 131→ // so we only need {client}/{stem}.html (no leading "transcripts/") 132→ return transcriptUrlFromRoot(m).replace(/^transcripts\//, '') 133→} 134→
